     finite zero-or-normal result, no overflow" ;

gaps.snippet:
const DOT_CITATION : & str = "recursive binary64 summation of exact products: each x_i*y_i is exact \
     in f64 (operands checked exact on the F32/BF16 grid, so significands are <= 24 bits — products \
     need <= 48 <= 53 — and product exponents lie in binary64's normal range), and the (dim−1)-add \
     recursive sum satisfies |fl(Σ) − Σ| <= γ_{dim−1}·Σ|x_i·y_i| with γ_k = k·u/(1−k·u), u = 2^−53 \
     — Higham, Accuracy and Stability of Numerical Algorithms (2002), §4.2 + Thm 2.2; additions \
     that underflow are exact (Hauser), so no subnormal side-condition arises. The stored ε = \
     1.05·dim·u·Σ̂ (Σ̂ = the computed abs-product sum) over-covers γ_{dim−1}·Σ|x_i·y_i| for every \
     dim < 2^32: the ×1.05 slack absorbs Σ̂'s own summation rounding and the ε-formula's evaluation \
     rounding. Side-conditions checked: per-element (exact on-grid finite inputs) and on the result \
     (finite — overflow is unreachable for dim < 2^32 since every |product| < 2^257)" ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const DOT_CITATION` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

gaps.snippet:
const SIMILARITY_CITATION : & str = "cosine dot/(‖a‖·‖b‖) in binary64 over exact products (see \
     dense.dot): |computed − true| <= 2.1·(dim+2)·u, u = 2^−53 — first-order sum of the numerator \
     term γ_{dim−1} (|fl(Σx_i·y_i) − Σ| <= γ_{dim−1}·Σ|x_i·y_i| <= γ_{dim−1}·‖a‖·‖b‖ by \
     Cauchy–Schwarz) and the denominator/quotient term (two positive-term norm sums + sqrt + \
     product + quotient roundings, <= (dim+3)·u to first order) times |cos| <= 1 — Higham (2002) \
     §4.2 + Thm 2.2; the slack in 2.1·(dim+2) over the first-order (2·dim+2)·u absorbs all \
     second-order γ-terms for dim < 2^32 and the ε-formula's own rounding. A zero-norm operand \
     returns the documented convention 0 exactly (an operand norm is 0 iff that operand is the \
     zero vector: squares of nonzero on-grid elements are >= 2^−298, so they cannot underflow to 0 \
     in f64). Side-conditions checked: per-element (exact on-grid finite inputs) and on the result \
     (finite)" ;
gaps.reason: top-level `const SIMILARITY_CITATION` — co-emit (ORACLE-R1 A4) only covers plain unsigned integer types (`u8`/`u16`/`u32`/`u64`/`u128`/`usize`); other types have no const item production and no faithful zero-arg-fn encoding (gap, never fabricate)

refused, never silently re-rounded."] NotOnGrid { # [doc = " Index of the offending element."] index : usize , } , # [doc = " The scale factor is non-finite or off the dtype grid (same contract as the elements)."] ScalarOffGrid , # [doc = " A result element is subnormal — outside the cited theorem's side-conditions (v1 scope,"] # [doc = " same honest refusal as M-211)."] SubnormalUnsupported { # [doc = " Index of the offending element."] index : usize , } , # [doc = " A result element overflows the dtype's finite range — explicit, never a silent ±Inf."] Overflow { # [doc = " Index of the offending element."] index : usize , } , # [doc = " The source value is itself approximate; composing its bound with the op's rounding ε is"] # [doc = " not a defined rule yet (recorded at M-204/M-211) — refused, never fabricated."] ApproximateSource , # [doc = " A constructed result violated a Core IR invariant."] Wf (WfError) , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Round an `f32` to the nearest bfloat16 (ties to even), widened back to `f32` bit-exactly —"] # [doc = " the same grid the M-211 swap targets. Caller has excluded NaN/Inf."] fn round_f32_to_bf16 (x : f32) -> f32 { let bits = x . to_bits () ; let lsb = (bits >> 16) & 1 ; f32 :: from_bits (((bits + 0x7FFF + lsb) >> 16) << 16) }
gaps.reason: `f32` has no confirmed Mycelium representation — `Float` (docs/spec/grammar/mycelium.ebnf:251) is IEEE-754 binary64 only at introduction (ADR-040 FLAG-1/M-897); a width extension is a future, separately-decided append, never silently assumed (VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Whether `x` is exactly representable on the `dtype` grid (finite values only)."] fn on_grid (dtype : ScalarKind , x : f64) -> bool { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let xf = x as f32 ; if f64 :: from (xf) != x { return false ; } match dtype { ScalarKind :: F32 => true , ScalarKind :: Bf16 => round_f32_to_bf16 (xf) == xf , ScalarKind :: F16 | ScalarKind :: F64 => false , } }
gaps.reason: PENDING-DESIGN(CU-3-fidelity): cast `x as f32` crosses the Binary/Float boundary — Rust `as` is lossy here (float->int *saturates*, int->float *rounds*), but the CU-3 kernel prims are checked/refusing (`flt.to_bin` refuses out-of-range; `bin.to_flt` errs |n| > 2^53 — ADR-040 §2.4), so no faithful prim exists. The faithful form is the reified lossy swap (ADR-040 §2.4/§5, NOT a prim), which the transpiler cannot emit yet — explicit gap (G2/VR-5)

`Proven`, never `Exact`: the recursive sum rounds)."] # [must_use] pub fn op_guarantee (op : DenseOp) -> GuaranteeStrength { match op { DenseOp :: Neg => GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , DenseOp :: Add | DenseOp :: Sub | DenseOp :: Scale | DenseOp :: Dot | DenseOp :: Similarity => { GuaranteeStrength :: Proven } } } # [doc = " The per-element relative ε this space's rounding ops carry."] # [must_use] pub fn op_rel_eps (& self) -> f64 { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_REL_EPS , _ => F32_OP_REL_EPS , } } fn op_citation (& self) -> & 'static str { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_CITATION , _ => F32_OP_CITATION , } } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** (ℓ∞ over the single result element) error bound of"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot_value`], given `abs_sum` = the computed `fl(Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|)`:"] # [doc = " `1.05 · dim · u · abs_sum` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the"] # [doc = " summation bound `γ_{dim−1}·Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|` (products are exact; see [`DOT_CITATION`] via the"] # [doc = " result's `ProvenThm` basis). Deliberately **not** [`Self::op_rel_eps`]: the dtype's"] # [doc = " per-element rounding ε never enters (inputs are exact on-grid, accumulation is binary64),"] # [doc = " and a *relative* claim on a dot product is false under cancellation (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn dot_abs_eps (& self , abs_sum : f64) -> f64 { 1.05 * f64 :: from (self . dim) * F64_ACC_U * abs_sum } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** error bound of [`Self::similarity_value`]:"] # [doc = " `2.1 · (dim + 2) · u` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the first-order"] # [doc = " bound `(2·dim + 2)·u` (numerator summation over exact products, via Cauchy–Schwarz, plus"] # [doc = " the norm/sqrt/quotient roundings against `|cos| ≤ 1`; see the result's `ProvenThm`"] # [doc = " citation). Input-independent: cosine's normalization caps the absolute error by"] # [doc = " construction."] # [must_use] pub fn similarity_abs_eps (& self) -> f64 { 2.1 * (f64 :: from (self . dim) + 2.0) * F64_ACC_U } # [doc = " Construct an **`Exact`** Dense value, checking every element is finite and exactly on the"] # [doc = " dtype grid — an off-grid payload would contradict its own `Repr` (refused, never"] # [doc = " re-rounded)."] pub fn value (& self , xs : Vec < f64 >) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . check_elements (& xs) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (xs) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } fn check_elements (& self , xs : & [f64]) -> Result < () , DenseError > { if xs . len () != self . dim as usize { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : u32 :: try_from (xs . len ()) . unwrap_or (u32 :: MAX) , }) ; } for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { if ! x . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: NonFinite { index }) ; } if ! on_grid (self . dtype , x) { return Err (DenseError :: NotOnGrid { index }) ; } } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Extract the scalar payload of a value belonging to this space, re-checking the contract"] # [doc = " the ops rely on (right space, `Exact` source, on-grid elements)."] fn scalars_of < 'a > (& self , v : & 'a Value) -> Result < & 'a [f64] , DenseError > { let Repr :: Dense { dim , dtype } = * v . repr () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; if dtype != self . dtype { return Err (DenseError :: DtypeMismatch { expected : self . dtype , }) ; } if dim != self . dim { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : dim , }) ; } if v . meta () . guarantee () != GuaranteeStrength :: Exact { return Err (DenseError :: ApproximateSource) ; } let Payload :: Scalars (xs) = v . payload () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; self . check_elements (xs) ? ; Ok (xs) } # [doc = " One elementwise result under the theorem's checked side-conditions: compute as a native"] # [doc = " `f32` op, round to the dtype grid, and refuse (explicitly) any element the cited bound"] # [doc = " does not cover."] fn round_result (& self , y32 : f32 , index : usize) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { if ! y32 . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if y32 != 0.0 && f64 :: from (y32 . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } let rounded = match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => { let r = round_f32_to_bf16 (y32) ; if ! r . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if r != 0.0 && f64 :: from (r . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } r } _ => y32 , } ; Ok (f64 :: from (rounded)) } # [doc = " Wrap a rounded result with its honest `Proven` rounding bound (M-I2: the basis is the"] # [doc = " checked theorem instantiation, never an assertion)."] fn wrap_proven (& self , data : Vec < f64 > , op : & str , inputs : Vec < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps : self . op_rel_eps () , norm : NormKind :: Rel , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : self . op_citation () . to_owned () , } , } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash (op) , inputs , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , Some (bound) , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (data) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a + b` (**`Proven`**, per-element relative ε — see crate docs)."] pub fn add_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.add" , | x , y | x + y) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a − b` (**`Proven`**, same bound as `add`)."] pub fn sub_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.sub" , | x , y | x - y) } fn elementwise (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value , op : & str , f : impl Fn (f32 , f32) -> f32 ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , (& x , & y)) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = f (x as f32 , y as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , op , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " Elementwise negation (**`Exact`** — the grids are symmetric, so no element ever rounds)."] pub fn neg_value (& self , a : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let out : Vec < f64 > = xs . iter () . map (| & x | - x) . collect () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash ("dense.neg") , inputs : vec ! [a . content_hash ()] , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (out) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Scalar multiplication `c · a` (**`Proven`**). `c` must be finite and on the dtype grid —"] # [doc = " the same contract as the elements (else [`DenseError::ScalarOffGrid`])."] pub fn scale_value (& self , a : & Value , c : f64) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { if ! c . is_finite () || ! on_grid (self . dtype , c) { return Err (DenseError :: ScalarOffGrid) ; } let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = (c as f32) * (x as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , "dense.scale" , vec ! [a . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " The shared measurement accumulation: the recursive (left-to-right) binary64 sum of the"] # [doc = " products and of their absolute values. Every product is **exact** in f64 (both factors"] # [doc = " are on the F32/BF16 grid — `scalars_of` has checked them), so the returned sums carry"] # [doc = " only the recursive-summation rounding the `DOT_CITATION` bound covers."] fn dot_sums (xs : & [f64] , ys : & [f64]) -> (f64 , f64) { let mut sum = 0.0 ; let mut abs_sum = 0.0 ; for (& x , & y) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) { let p = x * y ; sum += p ; abs_sum += p . abs () ; } (sum , abs_sum) } # [doc = " The shared cosine computation (`0` by documented convention if either norm is `0` — which"] # [doc = " happens iff that operand is exactly the zero vector; see `SIMILARITY_CITATION`)."] fn cosine (xs : & [f64] , ys : & [f64]) -> f64 { let (dot , _) = Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) ; let na : f64 = xs . iter () . map (| x | x * x) . sum :: < f64 > () . sqrt () ; let nb : f64 = ys . iter () . map (| x | x * x) . sum :: < f64 > () . sqrt () ; if na == 0.0 || nb == 0.0 { 0.0 } else { dot / (na * nb) } } # [doc = " Dot product in `f64` — a *measurement* helper (no `Meta` to tag), mirroring"] # [doc = " `VsaModel::similarity`. Typed errors for space mismatches. The value-returning twin"] # [doc = " with the honest `Proven` bound attached is [`Self::dot_value`] (M-891)."] pub fn dot (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; Ok (Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) . 0) } # [doc = " Cosine similarity in `[-1, 1]` (`0` if either operand has zero norm) — a measurement"] # [doc = " helper in `f64`. The value-returning twin with the honest `Proven` bound attached is"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity_value`] (M-891)."] pub fn similarity (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; Ok (Self :: cosine (xs , ys)) } # [doc = " Wrap a scalar `f64` measurement as a **`Dense{1, F64}`** result `Value` carrying its"] # [doc = " honest `Proven` absolute (`Linf` over the single element) error bound (M-891). The"] # [doc = " payload is the `f64` **exactly as computed** — never re-rounded onto the operand dtype"] # [doc = " grid (that would add error and spurious overflow/subnormal refusals to a measurement)."] # [doc = " `F64` has no Dense op set (v1 scope), so a measurement cannot silently feed back into"] # [doc = " dense arithmetic — re-entry would be an explicit `UnsupportedDtype` refusal (G2)."] fn wrap_measurement (& self , y : f64 , eps : f64 , citation : & 'static str , op : & str , inputs : Vec < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { if ! y . is_finite () || ! eps . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index : 0 }) ; } let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : NormKind :: Linf , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : citation . to_owned () , } , } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash (op) , inputs , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , Some (bound) , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (Repr :: Dense { dim : 1 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F64 , } , Payload :: Scalars (vec ! [y]) , meta ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Dot product as a **`Dense{1, F64}` value** with its honest per-op tag (M-891):"] # [doc = " **`Proven`**, absolute (`Linf`) ε = [`Self::dot_abs_eps`] of the computed abs-product"] # [doc = " sum, `ProvenThm` basis (binary64 summation over exact products — the citation carries"] # [doc = " the full argument and the checked side-conditions). Same operand contract as"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot`] (equal dim + dtype, `Exact` on-grid sources — typed errors otherwise)."] pub fn dot_value (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let (sum , abs_sum) = Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) ; self . wrap_measurement (sum , self . dot_abs_eps (abs_sum) , DOT_CITATION , "dense.dot" , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()] ,) } # [doc = " Cosine similarity as a **`Dense{1, F64}` value** with its honest per-op tag (M-891):"] # [doc = " **`Proven`**, absolute (`Linf`) ε = [`Self::similarity_abs_eps`] (input-independent —"] # [doc = " normalization caps the absolute error), `ProvenThm` basis. Zero-norm operands yield the"] # [doc = " documented convention `0` exactly (disclosed in the citation). Same operand contract as"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity`]."] pub fn similarity_value (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; self . wrap_measurement (Self :: cosine (xs , ys) , self . similarity_abs_eps () , SIMILARITY_CITATION , "dense.similarity" , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()] ,) } }

see [`DOT_CITATION`] via the"] # [doc = " result's `ProvenThm` basis). Deliberately **not** [`Self::op_rel_eps`]: the dtype's"] # [doc = " per-element rounding ε never enters (inputs are exact on-grid, accumulation is binary64),"] # [doc = " and a *relative* claim on a dot product is false under cancellation (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn dot_abs_eps (& self , abs_sum : f64) -> f64 { 1.05 * f64 :: from (self . dim) * F64_ACC_U * abs_sum } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** error bound of [`Self::similarity_value`]:"] # [doc = " `2.1 · (dim + 2) · u` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the first-order"] # [doc = " bound `(2·dim + 2)·u` (numerator summation over exact products, via Cauchy–Schwarz, plus"] # [doc = " the norm/sqrt/quotient roundings against `|cos| ≤ 1`; see the result's `ProvenThm`"] # [doc = " citation). ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (xs) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } fn check_elements (& self , xs : & [f64]) -> Result < () , DenseError > { if xs . len () != self . dim as usize { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : u32 :: try_from (xs . len ()) . unwrap_or (u32 :: MAX) , }) ; } for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { if ! x . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: NonFinite { index }) ; } if ! on_grid (self . dtype , x) { return Err (DenseError :: NotOnGrid { index }) ; } } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Extract the scalar payload of a value belonging to this space, re-checking the contract"] # [doc = " the ops rely on (right space, `Exact` source, on-grid elements)."] fn scalars_of < 'a > (& self , v : & 'a Value) -> Result < & 'a [f64] , DenseError > { let Repr :: Dense { dim , dtype } = * v . repr () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; if dtype != self . dtype { return Err (DenseError :: DtypeMismatch { expected : self . dtype , }) ; } if dim != self . dim { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : dim , }) ; } if v . meta () . guarantee () != GuaranteeStrength :: Exact { return Err (DenseError :: ApproximateSource) ; } let Payload :: Scalars (xs) = v . payload () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; self . check_elements (xs) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (data) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a + b` (**`Proven`**, per-element relative ε — see crate docs)."] pub fn add_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.add" , | x , y | x + y) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a − b` (**`Proven`**, same bound as `add`)."] pub fn sub_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.sub" , | x , y | x - y) } fn elementwise (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value , op : & str , f : impl Fn (f32 , f32) -> f32 ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " Elementwise negation (**`Exact`** — the grids are symmetric, so no element ever rounds)."] pub fn neg_value (& self , a : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let out : Vec < f64 > = xs . iter () . map (| & x | - x) . collect () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash ("dense.neg") , inputs : vec ! `add`/`sub`/`scale`"] # [doc = " round once (or twice for BF16) under the cited theorem (`Proven`); `dot`/`similarity`"] # [doc = " carry the proven binary64 accumulation bound (M-891 — see [`Self::dot_abs_eps`]/"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity_abs_eps`]; `Proven`, never `Exact`: the recursive sum rounds)."] # [must_use] pub fn op_guarantee (op : DenseOp) -> GuaranteeStrength { match op { DenseOp :: Neg => GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , DenseOp :: Add | DenseOp :: Sub | DenseOp :: Scale | DenseOp :: Dot | DenseOp :: Similarity => { GuaranteeStrength :: Proven } } } # [doc = " The per-element relative ε this space's rounding ops carry."] # [must_use] pub fn op_rel_eps (& self) -> f64 { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_REL_EPS , _ => F32_OP_REL_EPS , } } fn op_citation (& self) -> & 'static str { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_CITATION , _ => F32_OP_CITATION , } } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** (ℓ∞ over the single result element) error bound of"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot_value`], given `abs_sum` = the computed `fl(Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|)`:"] # [doc = " `1.05 · dim · u · abs_sum` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the"] # [doc = " summation bound `γ_{dim−1}·Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|` (products are exact; see [`DOT_CITATION`] via the"] # [doc = " result's `ProvenThm` basis). Deliberately **not** [`Self::op_rel_eps`]: the dtype's"] # [doc = " per-element rounding ε never enters (inputs are exact on-grid, accumulation is binary64),"] # [doc = " and a *relative* claim on a dot product is false under cancellation (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn dot_abs_eps (& self , abs_sum : f64) -> f64 { 1.05 * f64 :: from (self . dim) * F64_ACC_U * abs_sum } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** error bound of [`Self::similarity_value`]:"] # [doc = " `2.1 · (dim + 2) · u` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the first-order"] # [doc = " bound `(2·dim + 2)·u` (numerator summation over exact products, via Cauchy–Schwarz, plus"] # [doc = " the norm/sqrt/quotient roundings against `|cos| ≤ 1`; see the result's `ProvenThm`"] # [doc = " citation). Input-independent: cosine's normalization caps the absolute error by"] # [doc = " construction."] # [must_use] pub fn similarity_abs_eps (& self) -> f64 { 2.1 * (f64 :: from (self . dim) + 2.0) * F64_ACC_U } # [doc = " Construct an **`Exact`** Dense value, checking every element is finite and exactly on the"] # [doc = " dtype grid — an off-grid payload would contradict its own `Repr` (refused, never"] # [doc = " re-rounded)."] pub fn value (& self , xs : Vec < f64 >) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . check_elements (& xs) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (xs) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } fn check_elements (& self , xs : & [f64]) -> Result < () , DenseError > { if xs . len () != self . dim as usize { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : u32 :: try_from (xs . len ()) . unwrap_or (u32 :: MAX) , }) ; } for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { if ! x . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: NonFinite { index }) ; } if ! on_grid (self . dtype , x) { return Err (DenseError :: NotOnGrid { index }) ; } } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Extract the scalar payload of a value belonging to this space, re-checking the contract"] # [doc = " the ops rely on (right space, `Exact` source, on-grid elements)."] fn scalars_of < 'a > (& self , v : & 'a Value) -> Result < & 'a [f64] , DenseError > { let Repr :: Dense { dim , dtype } = * v . repr () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; if dtype != self . dtype { return Err (DenseError :: DtypeMismatch { expected : self . dtype , }) ; } if dim != self . dim { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : dim , }) ; } if v . meta () . guarantee () != GuaranteeStrength :: Exact { return Err (DenseError :: ApproximateSource) ; } let Payload :: Scalars (xs) = v . payload () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; self . check_elements (xs) ? ; Ok (xs) } # [doc = " One elementwise result under the theorem's checked side-conditions: compute as a native"] # [doc = " `f32` op, round to the dtype grid, and refuse (explicitly) any element the cited bound"] # [doc = " does not cover."] fn round_result (& self , y32 : f32 , index : usize) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { if ! y32 . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if y32 != 0.0 && f64 :: from (y32 . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } let rounded = match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => { let r = round_f32_to_bf16 (y32) ; if ! r . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if r != 0.0 && f64 :: from (r . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } r } _ => y32 , } ; Ok (f64 :: from (rounded)) } # [doc = " Wrap a rounded result with its honest `Proven` rounding bound (M-I2: the basis is the"] # [doc = " checked theorem instantiation, never an assertion)."] fn wrap_proven (& self , data : Vec < f64 > , op : & str , inputs : Vec < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps : self . op_rel_eps () , norm : NormKind :: Rel , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : self . op_citation () . to_owned () , } , } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash (op) , inputs , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , Some (bound) , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (data) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a + b` (**`Proven`**, per-element relative ε — see crate docs)."] pub fn add_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.add" , | x , y | x + y) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a − b` (**`Proven`**, same bound as `add`)."] pub fn sub_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.sub" , | x , y | x - y) } fn elementwise (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value , op : & str , f : impl Fn (f32 , f32) -> f32 ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , (& x , & y)) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = f (x as f32 , y as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , op , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " Elementwise negation (**`Exact`** — the grids are symmetric, so no element ever rounds)."] pub fn neg_value (& self , a : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let out : Vec < f64 > = xs . iter () . map (| & x | - x) . collect () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash ("dense.neg") , inputs : vec ! [a . content_hash ()] , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (out) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Scalar multiplication `c · a` (**`Proven`**). `c` must be finite and on the dtype grid —"] # [doc = " the same contract as the elements (else [`DenseError::ScalarOffGrid`])."] pub fn scale_value (& self , a : & Value , c : f64) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { if ! c . is_finite () || ! on_grid (self . dtype , c) { return Err (DenseError :: ScalarOffGrid) ; } let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = (c as f32) * (x as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , "dense.scale" , vec ! [a . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " The shared measurement accumulation: the recursive (left-to-right) binary64 sum of the"] # [doc = " products and of their absolute values. Every product is **exact** in f64 (both factors"] # [doc = " are on the F32/BF16 grid — `scalars_of` has checked them), so the returned sums carry"] # [doc = " only the recursive-summation rounding the `DOT_CITATION` bound covers."] fn dot_sums (xs : & [f64] , ys : & [f64]) -> (f64 , f64) { let mut sum = 0.0 ; let mut abs_sum = 0.0 ; for (& x , & y) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) { let p = x * y ; sum += p ; abs_sum += p . abs () ; } (sum , abs_sum) } # [doc = " The shared cosine computation (`0` by documented convention if either norm is `0` — which"] # [doc = " happens iff that operand is exactly the zero vector; see `SIMILARITY_CITATION`)."] fn cosine (xs : & [f64] , ys : & [f64]) -> f64 { let (dot , _) = Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) ; let na : f64 = xs . iter () . map (| x | x * x) . sum :: < f64 > () . sqrt () ; let nb : f64 = ys . iter () . map (| x | x * x) . sum :: < f64 > () . sqrt () ; if na == 0.0 || nb == 0.0 { 0.0 } else { dot / (na * nb) } } # [doc = " Dot product in `f64` — a *measurement* helper (no `Meta` to tag), mirroring"] # [doc = " `VsaModel::similarity`. Typed errors for space mismatches. The value-returning twin"] # [doc = " with the honest `Proven` bound attached is [`Self::dot_value`] (M-891)."] pub fn dot (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; Ok (Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) . 0) } # [doc = " Cosine similarity in `[-1, 1]` (`0` if either operand has zero norm) — a measurement"] # [doc = " helper in `f64`. The value-returning twin with the honest `Proven` bound attached is"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity_value`] (M-891)."] pub fn similarity (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; Ok (Self :: cosine (xs , ys)) } # [doc = " Wrap a scalar `f64` measurement as a **`Dense{1, F64}`** result `Value` carrying its"] # [doc = " honest `Proven` absolute (`Linf` over the single element) error bound (M-891). The"] # [doc = " payload is the `f64` **exactly as computed** — never re-rounded onto the operand dtype"] # [doc = " grid (that would add error and spurious overflow/subnormal refusals to a measurement)."] # [doc = " `F64` has no Dense op set (v1 scope), so a measurement cannot silently feed back into"] # [doc = " dense arithmetic — re-entry would be an explicit `UnsupportedDtype` refusal (G2)."] fn wrap_measurement (& self , y : f64 , eps : f64 , citation : & 'static str , op : & str , inputs : Vec < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { if ! y . is_finite () || ! eps . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index : 0 }) ; } let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps , norm : NormKind :: Linf , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : citation . to_owned () , } , } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash (op) , inputs , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , Some (bound) , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (Repr :: Dense { dim : 1 , dtype : ScalarKind :: F64 , } , Payload :: Scalars (vec ! [y]) , meta ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Dot product as a **`Dense{1, F64}` value** with its honest per-op tag (M-891):"] # [doc = " **`Proven`**, absolute (`Linf`) ε = [`Self::dot_abs_eps`] of the computed abs-product"] # [doc = " sum, `ProvenThm` basis (binary64 summation over exact products — the citation carries"] # [doc = " the full argument and the checked side-conditions). Same operand contract as"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot`] (equal dim + dtype, `Exact` on-grid sources — typed errors otherwise)."] pub fn dot_value (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let (sum , abs_sum) = Self :: dot_sums (xs , ys) ; self . wrap_measurement (sum , self . dot_abs_eps (abs_sum) , DOT_CITATION , "dense.dot" , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()] ,) } # [doc = " Cosine similarity as a **`Dense{1, F64}` value** with its honest per-op tag (M-891):"] # [doc = " **`Proven`**, absolute (`Linf`) ε = [`Self::similarity_abs_eps`] (input-independent —"] # [doc = " normalization caps the absolute error), `ProvenThm` basis. Zero-norm operands yield the"] # [doc = " documented convention `0` exactly (disclosed in the citation). Same operand contract as"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity`]."] pub fn similarity_value (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; self . wrap_measurement (Self :: cosine (xs , ys) , self . similarity_abs_eps () , SIMILARITY_CITATION , "dense.similarity" , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()] ,) } }

`add`/`sub`/`scale`"] # [doc = " round once (or twice for BF16) under the cited theorem (`Proven`); `dot`/`similarity`"] # [doc = " carry the proven binary64 accumulation bound (M-891 — see [`Self::dot_abs_eps`]/"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity_abs_eps`]; `Proven`, never `Exact`: the recursive sum rounds)."] # [must_use] pub fn op_guarantee (op : DenseOp) -> GuaranteeStrength { match op { DenseOp :: Neg => GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , DenseOp :: Add | DenseOp :: Sub | DenseOp :: Scale | DenseOp :: Dot | DenseOp :: Similarity => { GuaranteeStrength :: Proven } } } # [doc = " The per-element relative ε this space's rounding ops carry."] # [must_use] pub fn op_rel_eps (& self) -> f64 { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_REL_EPS , _ => F32_OP_REL_EPS , } } fn op_citation (& self) -> & 'static str { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_CITATION , _ => F32_OP_CITATION , } } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** (ℓ∞ over the single result element) error bound of"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot_value`], given `abs_sum` = the computed `fl(Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|)`:"] # [doc = " `1.05 · dim · u · abs_sum` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the"] # [doc = " summation bound `γ_{dim−1}·Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|` (products are exact; see [`DOT_CITATION`] via the"] # [doc = " result's `ProvenThm` basis). Deliberately **not** [`Self::op_rel_eps`]: the dtype's"] # [doc = " per-element rounding ε never enters (inputs are exact on-grid, accumulation is binary64),"] # [doc = " and a *relative* claim on a dot product is false under cancellation (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn dot_abs_eps (& self , abs_sum : f64) -> f64 { 1.05 * f64 :: from (self . dim) * F64_ACC_U * abs_sum } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** error bound of [`Self::similarity_value`]:"] # [doc = " `2.1 · (dim + 2) · u` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the first-order"] # [doc = " bound `(2·dim + 2)·u` (numerator summation over exact products, via Cauchy–Schwarz, plus"] # [doc = " the norm/sqrt/quotient roundings against `|cos| ≤ 1`; see the result's `ProvenThm`"] # [doc = " citation). Input-independent: cosine's normalization caps the absolute error by"] # [doc = " construction."] # [must_use] pub fn similarity_abs_eps (& self) -> f64 { 2.1 * (f64 :: from (self . dim) + 2.0) * F64_ACC_U } # [doc = " Construct an **`Exact`** Dense value, checking every element is finite and exactly on the"] # [doc = " dtype grid — an off-grid payload would contradict its own `Repr` (refused, never"] # [doc = " re-rounded)."] pub fn value (& self , xs : Vec < f64 >) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . check_elements (& xs) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (xs) , Meta :: exact (Provenance :: Root) ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } fn check_elements (& self , xs : & [f64]) -> Result < () , DenseError > { if xs . len () != self . dim as usize { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : u32 :: try_from (xs . len ()) . unwrap_or (u32 :: MAX) , }) ; } for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { if ! x . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: NonFinite { index }) ; } if ! on_grid (self . dtype , x) { return Err (DenseError :: NotOnGrid { index }) ; } } Ok (()) } # [doc = " Extract the scalar payload of a value belonging to this space, re-checking the contract"] # [doc = " the ops rely on (right space, `Exact` source, on-grid elements)."] fn scalars_of < 'a > (& self , v : & 'a Value) -> Result < & 'a [f64] , DenseError > { let Repr :: Dense { dim , dtype } = * v . repr () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; if dtype != self . dtype { return Err (DenseError :: DtypeMismatch { expected : self . dtype , }) ; } if dim != self . dim { return Err (DenseError :: DimMismatch { expected : self . dim , got : dim , }) ; } if v . meta () . guarantee () != GuaranteeStrength :: Exact { return Err (DenseError :: ApproximateSource) ; } let Payload :: Scalars (xs) = v . payload () else { return Err (DenseError :: NotDense) ; } ; self . check_elements (xs) ? ; Ok (xs) } # [doc = " One elementwise result under the theorem's checked side-conditions: compute as a native"] # [doc = " `f32` op, round to the dtype grid, and refuse (explicitly) any element the cited bound"] # [doc = " does not cover."] fn round_result (& self , y32 : f32 , index : usize) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { if ! y32 . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if y32 != 0.0 && f64 :: from (y32 . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } let rounded = match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => { let r = round_f32_to_bf16 (y32) ; if ! r . is_finite () { return Err (DenseError :: Overflow { index }) ; } if r != 0.0 && f64 :: from (r . abs ()) < DENSE_MIN_NORMAL { return Err (DenseError :: SubnormalUnsupported { index }) ; } r } _ => y32 , } ; Ok (f64 :: from (rounded)) } # [doc = " Wrap a rounded result with its honest `Proven` rounding bound (M-I2: the basis is the"] # [doc = " checked theorem instantiation, never an assertion)."] fn wrap_proven (& self , data : Vec < f64 > , op : & str , inputs : Vec < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let bound = Bound { kind : BoundKind :: Error { eps : self . op_rel_eps () , norm : NormKind :: Rel , } , basis : BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { citation : self . op_citation () . to_owned () , } , } ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash (op) , inputs , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , Some (bound) , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (data) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a + b` (**`Proven`**, per-element relative ε — see crate docs)."] pub fn add_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.add" , | x , y | x + y) } # [doc = " Elementwise `a − b` (**`Proven`**, same bound as `add`)."] pub fn sub_values (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { self . elementwise (a , b , "dense.sub" , | x , y | x - y) } fn elementwise (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value , op : & str , f : impl Fn (f32 , f32) -> f32 ,) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , (& x , & y)) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = f (x as f32 , y as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , op , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " Elementwise negation (**`Exact`** — the grids are symmetric, so no element ever rounds)."] pub fn neg_value (& self , a : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let out : Vec < f64 > = xs . iter () . map (| & x | - x) . collect () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash ("dense.neg") , inputs : vec ! [a . content_hash ()] , } , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , None , None , None , None ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) ? ; Value :: new (self . repr () , Payload :: Scalars (out) , meta) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Scalar multiplication `c · a` (**`Proven`**). `c` must be finite and on the dtype grid —"] # [doc = " the same contract as the elements (else [`DenseError::ScalarOffGrid`])."] pub fn scale_value (& self , a : & Value , c : f64) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { if ! c . is_finite () || ! on_grid (self . dtype , c) { return Err (DenseError :: ScalarOffGrid) ; } let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , & x) in xs . iter () . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = (c as f32) * (x as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , "dense.scale" , vec ! [a . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " The shared measurement accumulation: the recursive (left-to-right) binary64 sum of the"] # [doc = " products and of their absolute values. The value-returning twin with the honest `Proven` bound attached is"] # [doc = " [`Self::similarity_value`] (M-891)."] pub fn similarity (& self , a : & Value , b : & Value) -> Result < f64 , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; Ok (Self :: cosine (xs , ys)) } # [doc = " Wrap a scalar `f64` measurement as a **`Dense{1, F64}`** result `Value` carrying its"] # [doc = " honest `Proven` absolute (`Linf` over the single element) error bound (M-891). [y]) , meta ,) . map_err (DenseError :: Wf) } # [doc = " Dot product as a **`Dense{1, F64}` value** with its honest per-op tag (M-891):"] # [doc = " **`Proven`**, absolute (`Linf`) ε = [`Self::dot_abs_eps`] of the computed abs-product"] # [doc = " sum, `ProvenThm` basis (binary64 summation over exact products — the citation carries"] # [doc = " the full argument and the checked side-conditions). `Proven`, never `Exact`: the recursive sum rounds)."] # [must_use] pub fn op_guarantee (op : DenseOp) -> GuaranteeStrength { match op { DenseOp :: Neg => GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , DenseOp :: Add | DenseOp :: Sub | DenseOp :: Scale | DenseOp :: Dot | DenseOp :: Similarity => { GuaranteeStrength :: Proven } } } # [doc = " The per-element relative ε this space's rounding ops carry."] # [must_use] pub fn op_rel_eps (& self) -> f64 { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_REL_EPS , _ => F32_OP_REL_EPS , } } fn op_citation (& self) -> & 'static str { match self . dtype { ScalarKind :: Bf16 => BF16_OP_CITATION , _ => F32_OP_CITATION , } } # [doc = " The disclosed **absolute** (ℓ∞ over the single result element) error bound of"] # [doc = " [`Self::dot_value`], given `abs_sum` = the computed `fl(Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|)`:"] # [doc = " `1.05 · dim · u · abs_sum` with `u =` [`F64_ACC_U`] — a conservative cover of the"] # [doc = " summation bound `γ_{dim−1}·Σ|xᵢ·yᵢ|` (products are exact; ; let ys = self . scalars_of (b) ? ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (xs . len ()) ; for (index , (& x , & y)) in xs . iter () . zip (ys) . enumerate () { # [allow (clippy :: cast_possible_truncation)] let y32 = f (x as f32 , y as f32) ; out . push (self . round_result (y32 , index) ?) ; } self . wrap_proven (out , op , vec ! [a . content_hash () , b . content_hash ()]) } # [doc = " Elementwise negation (**`Exact`** — the grids are symmetric, so no element ever rounds)."] pub fn neg_value (& self , a : & Value) -> Result < Value , DenseError > { let xs = self . scalars_of (a) ? ; let out : Vec < f64 > = xs . iter () . map (| & x | - x) . collect () ; let meta = Meta :: new (Provenance :: Derived { op : operation_hash ("dense.neg") , inputs : vec !
